reason: Increasing stop loss from 1.5% to 2.0% gives trades more room to survive quick reversals, reducing the observed loss while preserving overall risk exposure.
Strategy performing well. Increasing position size by 5%.
Increase EMA fast length to smooth trend detection, aiming to improve Sharpe ratio.

Current average loss (~2.57%) exceeds the 2.0% stop loss. Reducing the stop loss to 1.5% cuts downside per trade, improves the risk-adjusted return (Sharpe) and helps keep drawdown under the 8% limit while preserving the existing profit target.

The loss was caused by an early stop-loss triggered after a rapid momentum reversal. Lowering the 1-hour RSI exit threshold from 40 to 35 will reduce premature exits, allowing trades more room to recover and increasing the likelihood of reaching the 4% take-profit target.
Increasing stop loss from 1.5% to 2.0% gives trades more room to survive quick reversals, reducing the observed loss while preserving overall risk exposure.
